name: Tortoises
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Mix sugar, margarine, and vanilla in a bowl until combined.
3. Stir in flour and salt until the dough just holds together. If the dough is too dry, add 1 to 2 tablespoons of water.
4. Place groups of three pecan halves on an ungreased cookie sheet at angles to each other.
5. Use teaspoons of dough to shape around a caramel piece for each group of nuts.
6. Bake until set but not brown, about 12 to 15 minutes.
7. Cool the cookies completely.
8. Dip the tops of the cooled cookies into Chocolate Glaze.
Chocolate Glaze:
1. In a bowl, beat together 1 cup powdered sugar, 1 teaspoon water, 1 ounce (1 square) unsweetened chocolate (melted), and 1 teaspoon vanilla until smooth.
2. If necessary, stir in water, 1 teaspoon at a time, until the glaze is of dipping consistency.
These cookies should be made at least 2 weeks before being eaten; they will keep for 4 to 6 weeks after that.
